help.title: Knowledge base
help.subtitle: Guides, shortcuts, privacy notes, and live system status for your household.

localFirst.question: What does “local-first” mean?
localFirst.answer: Recipes, pantry, meal plan, and shopping list live primarily in IndexedDB on your device. CulinaSync does not run a central cloud database for your kitchen — you keep control, speed, and offline use.

aiPrivacy.question: What happens to my data in AI Chef?
aiPrivacy.answer: Cloud AI (Google Gemini) receives only the text of your request — and only if you stored an API key. Pantry and planning data are not uploaded automatically. You can control context toggles in AI settings, use local AI (Ollama), or keep prompts on-device only (privacy).

apiKey.question: Do I need an API key?
apiKey.answer: For Gemini in the cloud: yes. The key is encrypted in IndexedDB on your device only — never in the build or on our servers. Without a key, cloud AI stays inactive; pantry, planner, and local features still work.

localAi.question: What is “local AI”?
localAi.answer: Under Settings → Local AI you configure Ollama, model routing, and optional GPU tiers. Requests can stay on-device — ideal for sensitive household data or offline labs. Cloud Gemini remains optional in parallel.

syncDevices.question: How do I sync between devices?
syncDevices.answer: Several paths: (1) QR device sync in Data & storage, (2) encrypted vault (.csb) for secure copy, (3) cloud sync via WebDAV/Nextcloud with merge, (4) classic JSON/Markdown export. Pick what fits your trust model and network.

vault.question: What is the encrypted vault (.csb)?
vault.answer: A password-protected snapshot of your kitchen — recipes, pantry, plan, and list in one file. Great for USB, NAS, or encrypted storage. Import merges or replaces per dialog; test with a backup before going live.

cloudSync.question: How does cloud sync (WebDAV/Nextcloud) work?
cloudSync.answer: You configure server, user, and path under Data & storage. Upload/download uses your own storage — CulinaSync does not host household data. After merge you see the last sync timestamp; conflicts follow the merge rules in the data panel.

policies.question: What are policies (allergens, blacklist) for?
policies.answer: Household rules filter recipes and AI suggestions: allergens, banned ingredients, minimum stock. In strict mode CulinaSync blocks risky suggestions early — especially helpful for families with intolerances.

pantryColor.question: What do meal planner colors mean?
pantryColor.answer: The bar shows ingredient availability from pantry: green (all set), yellow (some missing), red (many missing). Spot shopping needs at a glance — without opening the recipe list.

pwaInstall.question: How do I install CulinaSync as an app?
pwaInstall.answer: In Data & storage open the PWA card: Add to Home Screen (iOS) or the install prompt (Chrome/Edge). Installed apps run standalone, cache the UI shell, and stay usable offline.

backupFormats.question: Which backup format when?
backupFormats.answer: JSON: full restore. Markdown/CSV: read & share. PDF: archive/print. For device changes we recommend JSON or vault; for routine backup a weekly JSON export is often enough.

cookVoice.question: Can I control cooking by voice?
cookVoice.answer: Yes — under Speech & audio set TTS, Whisper (local), or browser recognition. Cook mode supports voice for timers and navigation; destructive commands can require confirmation.

onboarding.question: How do I restart the introduction?
onboarding.answer: In Help → Pro tips, choose “Open tour” (first card). The tour covers navigation, main content, and module switching — you can load demo pantry data again.

cmdPalette.title: Command palette
cmdPalette.description: ⌘K / Ctrl+K — jump to modules, settings, and actions without the mouse.

apiKey.title: Set up Gemini safely
apiKey.description: BYOK in IndexedDB — restrict the key in Google Cloud to your domain.

voiceControl.title: Hands-free at the stove
voiceControl.description: Tune voice, Whisper model, and confirmations for destructive commands.

shoppingGenerate.title: Shop from the plan
shoppingGenerate.description: Push missing ingredients from the meal plan to the list in one step.

mealPlanner.title: Plan the week like a pro
mealPlanner.description: Colored pantry bars + servings — review the plan before shopping.

aiChef.title: AI Chef with context
aiChef.description: Combine creativity, diet, and policies — then ask the chef.

onboardingReplay.title: Replay onboarding
onboardingReplay.description: Re-run the welcome tour covering header, main area, and bottom navigation anytime.

offlineFirst.title: Offline first
offlineFirst.description: Kitchen works without network — sync is additive, not required.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Your data stays on device; cloud only with your key and your server.

performance.title: Instant feel
performance.description: Dexie + local UI — no waiting on a remote household server.

trust.title: Trust
trust.description: Clear toggles instead of hidden telemetry — you choose what is stored.

open.title: Open exports
open.description: JSON, Markdown, CSV — no lock-in for your recipes.

sync.title: Sync on demand
sync.description: QR, vault, or WebDAV — pick what fits your home.

about.lead: CulinaSync is your local-first kitchen assistant: pantry, planning, cooking, and shopping — optionally with cloud or local AI, always with exportable data.

whatsNew.title: What's new?
whatsNew.description: Overview of the most important changes in this version.

items.audit: Universal audit: performance, security, accessibility, best practices
items.seo: SEO/meta/OG/Twitter, preload, print styles, version badge

entry.loaded: Demo pantry items have been loaded.
entry.try: Started empty — your data stays on this device only.

banner.message: New on GitHub Pages? Load sample pantry data and try CulinaSync with no setup.
banner.loaded: {{count}} demo items added to your pantry.

onboarding.title: Welcome to CulinaSync!
onboarding.description: Start with a guided tour or load demo data for a hands-on tutorial.

tour.title: Guided tour
tour.description: Learn navigation and core areas in 3 steps.
tour.step1: Up here you find navigation, voice control, and quick access to settings.
tour.step2: Here you work in the current area: Pantry, Recipes, Planner, or Shopping List.
tour.step3: Down here you can quickly switch between modules in your daily cooking routine.

demo.title: Interactive tutorial
demo.description: Fills your pantry with sample items so you can test immediately.

localAi.title: Local AI (default)
localAi.description: Recipe ideas and RAG run local-first — cloud optional via Gemini API key.

localAiSetup.title: Set up local AI
localAiSetup.description: CulinaSync uses local-first by default — no API key required. Optionally enable WebLLM and embeddings.
localAiSetup.gpuWasm: No WebGPU — Transformers.js runs via WASM (slightly slower).
localAiSetup.offlineDesc: Heuristics (layer 4) are always available; RAG uses recipes, pantry, and meal plan.
localAiSetup.hint: You can adjust everything anytime under Settings → Local AI.
localAiSetup.panelDesc: Configure GPU check, embeddings, and WebLLM in a few steps.
